task_completion.description: Verify that the 120g electronics payload was not deleted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenRocket component tree, the 'Electronics Payload' (or similar 120g mass) should still be visible in the aft section of the rocket.

task_completion.description: Verify that a new mass component (ballast) was added to the nose cone or upper body tube.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenRocket component tree, a new Mass Component (e.g., 'Mass component', 'Nose weight', 'Ballast') should be visible as a child of the Nose Cone or the uppermost Body Tube. Ignore the pre-existing 'Wadding' component.

task_completion.description: Verify that the stability margin is at least 1.5 calibers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenRocket design window (usually top right), the 'Stability:' indicator should read 1.50 cal or higher.

task_completion.description: Verify that a flight simulation was run after modifications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight Simulations' tab in OpenRocket should be visible, showing a completed simulation run with populated results for 'Max. altitude' and 'Ground hit velocity'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the mass budget report was written with all required details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal should show the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/exports/mass_budget_report.txt'. The text must include: the stability issue, the ballast mass and location, the final stability margin, and the simulation results (ground hit velocity and max altitude).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the design and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the OpenRocket application open with the rocket design loaded, rather than the agent just editing the .ork XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify that the simulation results in the report match the actual simulation run.
integrity.visual_evidence: The max altitude and ground hit velocity values written in the text report should match the values visible in the OpenRocket 'Flight Simulations' tab.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually added new ballast and didn't just exploit pre-existing components.
integrity.visual_evidence: The component tree should show a genuinely new mass component added to the front of the rocket, not just renaming or using the pre-existing 0.002kg 'Wadding'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/mass_audit_rocket.ork'. The agent must NOT delete the injected 120g (0.12 kg) electronics payload. The agent must add a new mass component (ballast) to the nose cone or upper body tube to achieve a stability margin of >= 1.5 calibers. Note: The original file contains a 'Wadding' mass of 0.002 kg; this is NOT the ballast. The agent must add new ballast (typically between 0.03 kg and 0.3 kg). The final report must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/mass_budget_report.txt' and include the stability issue, ballast details, final stability margin, and simulation results (ground hit velocity, max altitude).
